meta_title: For Freelancers: debugging with AI to Solve slow development speed (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to debugging with AI and overcome slow development speed. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Debugging with AI: Speed Up Your Freelance Dev Workflow
content.seo_description: Stop wasting hours on bugs. Use this intermediate checklist to integrate Antigravity and Claude Code into your workflow and ship client projects 3x faster.
content.answer_block: To eliminate slow development cycles, transition from manual log-reading to AI-driven diagnostic loops. By integrating Antigravity for real-time architectural analysis and Claude Code for CLI-based automated refactoring, you can identify, isolate, and patch bugs in minutes, transforming your freelance workflow from reactive firefighting into proactive, high-velocity delivery.
content.intro:
The clock is ticking on your client’s deadline, and your terminal is bleeding red errors. You’re stuck in the 'debugging trap'—spending 80% of your billable time deciphering stack traces instead of shipping features. It is the silent killer of freelance profitability, turning high-value projects into grueling marathons of frustration.

But the era of manual debugging is over. By leveraging autonomous AI agents and intelligent CLI tools, you can offload the cognitive heavy lifting of error identification. This guide provides the tactical blueprint to turn your terminal into a high-octane engine, allowing you to bypass bottlenecks and reclaim your development speed.

steps.title: Contextualize the Incident
steps.description: Before fixing, you must define the scope. Use AI to map the dependency tree of your error to prevent regression.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the current execution flow and identify the root cause of the recent stack trace. Map the affected module's dependencies and list potential side effects of a quick-fix patch.
steps.claude_code_command: claude read ./src/services/auth.ts --query 'Analyze the logic error in the token validation loop'

steps.title: Automated Diagnostic Loops
steps.description: Shift from guessing to deterministic testing by letting AI generate test cases that isolate the bug.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a suite of unit tests that recreate this specific bug in an isolated environment. Ensure the test fails under current conditions and passes once the fix is applied.

steps.title: Surgical Code Refactoring
steps.description: Apply precision fixes. Use Claude Code to implement the fix while maintaining existing style guidelines.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor the identified problematic function to handle edge cases where the input is null. Maintain the existing interface and performance constraints.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit ./src/services/auth.ts --instruction 'Apply the fix for null pointer exceptions in the token validation logic'

steps.title: Verification and Integration
steps.description: Validate the fix against the codebase, ensuring no downstream services were impacted by the changes.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Perform a static analysis on the modified file and its consumers. Verify that the changes align with the project's architectural patterns.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'npm run lint && npm run test'

steps.title: Documentation of the Fix
steps.description: Ensure future maintainability by documenting the bug fix directly into the codebase documentation.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Summarize the bug, the root cause, and the applied fix into a markdown file for the project's internal engineering log.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write ./docs/FIX_LOG.md --content 'Document the fix for auth-token edge case'

pro_tips: Always use the --diff flag in Claude Code to review AI-suggested changes before final commitment to your repository.
Treat your Antigravity prompts as 'system instructions'—include your project's coding style guide in the initial prompt for consistent output.
Use Claude Code's 'history' feature to audit your debugging session if you need to explain the fix to a client later.
Create a custom 'debug-context.md' file that the AI can read to understand your project's specific quirks and legacy debt.
Run your AI debugging sessions in a temporary git branch to keep your main branch clean and stable until the fix is battle-tested.
content.next_steps: After completing this workflow, begin building an automated 'pre-commit' hook that triggers your AI diagnostic suite whenever a test fails.
content.conversion_block: You now have the power to slash your debugging time by half. Stop fighting the code and start mastering it. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
